title: Land of Confusion
meaning_summary: Land of Confusion still makes money because its political-pop hook and recurring video-era recognition keep it visible in the Genesis catalog.
revenue_drivers: The song retains strong 1980s playlist and classic-rock crossover appeal.
Its recognizable chorus keeps replay healthy in legacy listening.
Writer-side economics improve the retained share of long-tail demand.

earnings.assumptions: Estimate assumes durable replay within the Genesis catalog, 1980s nostalgia demand, and moderate sync-style reuse.

ownership.catalog_sale_status: not publicly modeled as a fully sold-out song position
ownership.notes: Artist-side economics are modeled from likely band and writer participation, not disclosed royalty documents.
